title: JSON-LD, Structured Data, Open Graph, Social Metadata, and Non-Guarantees · Program Brief: Rich Result Nonclaim
summary: Malformed JSON-LD is silently accepted, remote contexts are fetched, structured claims conflict with visible content, and complete metadata is reported as a guaranteed rich result. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rich result nonclaim increment in structured and social metadata evidence report. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Distinguish syntax, schema, visible-content, vendor-eligibility, policy, rendering, and live search evidence and avoid ranking or display guarantees.
Audit Open Graph and other selected social fields by declared platform contract, URL identity, image evidence, locale, and fallback behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-structured-social-metadata-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-structured-social-metadata-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_structured_social_metadata_4(documents: int = 20, parsed_documents: int = 17, rejected_documents: int = 3, visible_conflicts: int = 4, remote_context_fetches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or parsed_documents < 0 or rejected_documents < 0:
        return False, documents, "metadata-count"
    if parsed_documents + rejected_documents != documents:
        return False, parsed_documents, "metadata-reconciliation"
    if visible_conflicts < 0:
        return False, visible_conflicts, "visible-content-evidence"
    if remote_context_fetches > 0:
        return False, remote_context_fetches, "remote-context-fetch"
    assert parsed_documents + rejected_documents == documents and remote_context_fetches == 0
    return True, parsed_documents, "bounded-structured-metadata"

# Evidence: scraper-rich-result-nonclaim-1pmh3f

def evidence_scraper_rich_result_nonclaim_1pmh3f(documents: int = 20, parsed_documents: int = 17, rejected_documents: int = 3, visible_conflicts: int = 4, remote_context_fetches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "url7144",
        "responsef3hmp1",
        "tree1pmh3f7144",
        "frontier71441pmh3f",

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Rich Result Nonclaim evidence · Theory Rich Result Nonclaim, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Rich Result Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rich Result Nonclaim model · Theory Rich Result Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rich Result Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 8050 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate bounded JSON-LD, schema and visible-content evidence, social metadata, and rich-result non-guarantees; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish syntax, schema, visible-content, vendor-eligibility, policy, rendering, and live search evidence and avoid ranking or display guarantees.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rich Result Nonclaim model · Theory Rich Result Nonclaim.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rich Result Nonclaim model · Theory Rich Result Nonclaim, keep Rich Result Nonclaim connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish syntax, schema, visible-content, vendor-eligibility, policy, rendering, and live search evidence and avoid ranking or display guarantees.
